ddls:
CREATE TABLE "genomic_sequences" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence */,
  "sequence_name" TEXT /* Name of the sequence */,
  "sequence_type" TEXT /* Type of sequence (e.g., DNA, RNA, protein) */,
  "organism" TEXT /* Organism the sequence belongs to */,
  "length" INTEGER /* Length of the sequence */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sequence */,
  "sequence_data" TEXT /* The actual sequence data */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* The date the sequence was added to the database */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* The date the sequence was last modified */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Whether the sequence is public or private */,
  "source" TEXT /* The source of the sequence data (e.g., laboratory, public database) */,
  "accession_number" TEXT /* The accession number if the sequence is from a public database */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genomic_sequences_sequence_type FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_type") REFERENCES sequence_types ("type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genomic_sequences_organism FOREIGN KEY ("organism") REFERENCES organisms ("organism_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "organisms" (
  "organism_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each organism */,
  "organism_name" TEXT /* Name of the organism */,
  "kingdom" TEXT /* Kingdom the organism belongs to */,
  "phylum" TEXT /* Phylum the organism belongs to */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class the organism belongs to */,
  "common_name" TEXT /* Common name of the organism */,
  "genetic_code" TEXT /* The genetic code used by the organism */,
  "genetic_map" TEXT /* The genetic map of the organism */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("organism_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sequence type (e.g., DNA, RNA, protein) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sequence type */,
  "is_nucleic_acid" INTEGER /* Whether the sequence type is a nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) */,
  "is_protein" INTEGER /* Whether the sequence type is a protein */,
  "default_length" INTEGER /* The default length for sequences of this type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_annotations" (
  "annotation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each annotation */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sequence the annotation belongs to */,
  "annotation_type" TEXT /* Type of annotation (e.g., gene, protein, repeat) */,
  "annotation_value" TEXT /* Value of the annotation */,
  "annotation_source" TEXT /* The source of the annotation (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "annotation_date" TEXT /* The date the annotation was added or last updated */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* The confidence score of the annotation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("annotation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_annotations_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES genomic_sequences ("sequence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comparison_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comparison method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the comparison method (e.g., BLAST, MUSCLE, ClustalW) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the comparison method */,
  "algorithm_version" TEXT /* The version of the algorithm used */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Whether the method is the default method for comparisons */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* Performance metrics of the method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_comparisons" (
  "comparison_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comparison */,
  "sequence_id1" INTEGER /* ID of the first sequence in the comparison */,
  "sequence_id2" INTEGER /* ID of the second sequence in the comparison */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the comparison method used */,
  "similarity" REAL /* Similarity between the two sequences */,
  "comparison_date" TEXT /* The date the comparison was performed */,
  "comparison_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the comparison */,
  "comparison_status" TEXT /* The status of the comparison (e.g., completed, in progress) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comparison_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_comparisons_sequence_id1 FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id1") REFERENCES genomic_sequences ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_comparisons_sequence_id2 FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id2") REFERENCES genomic_sequences ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_comparisons_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES comparison_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* The hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date the user last logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "institution" TEXT /* The institution the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sequence being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sequence */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sequence was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* The IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* The user agent string of the client */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Whether the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES genomic_sequences ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "organism_taxonomies" (
  "taxonomy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each taxonomic classification */,
  "organism_id" INTEGER /* ID of the organism the classification belongs to */,
  "kingdom" TEXT /* Kingdom the organism belongs to */,
  "phylum" TEXT /* Phylum the organism belongs to */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class the organism belongs to */,
  "order" TEXT /* Order the organism belongs to */,
  "family" TEXT /* Family the organism belongs to */,
  "genus" TEXT /* Genus the organism belongs to */,
  "species" TEXT /* Species the organism belongs to */,
  "subspecies" TEXT /* Subspecies of the organism */,
  "strain" TEXT /* Strain of the organism */,
  "is_type_strain" INTEGER /* Whether the organism is a type strain */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("taxonomy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_organism_taxonomies_organism_id FOREIGN KEY ("organism_id") REFERENCES organisms ("organism_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequence_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feature */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sequence the feature belongs to */,
  "feature_type" TEXT /* Type of feature (e.g., gene, repeat, regulatory region) */,
  "start_position" INTEGER /* Start position of the feature */,
  "end_position" INTEGER /* End position of the feature */,
  "feature_description" TEXT /* A description of the feature */,
  "feature_source" TEXT /* The source of the feature information */,
  "feature_confidence_score" REAL /* The confidence score of the feature */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequence_features_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES genomic_sequences ("sequence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publications" (
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publication */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sequence the publication is related to */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the publication */,
  "authors" TEXT /* Authors of the publication */,
  "journal" TEXT /* Journal where the publication was published */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the publication was published */,
  "doi" TEXT /* The DOI of the publication */,
  "url" TEXT /* The URL to access the publication */,
  "citation_count" INTEGER /* The number of times the publication has been cited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publications_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES genomic_sequences ("sequence_id")
)
